response.justification: While the candidate exceeds the experience threshold with 14+ years of professional work and demonstrates strong systems-thinking across full-stack development, the resume shows no evidence of the specialized domain expertise required for this role: SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, hardware-software co-design for accelerators, or deep-learning workload optimization. Performance optimization experience is primarily application and infrastructure-level rather than hardware-focused. This is a fundamental mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect role where domain expertise is non-negotiable.
strengths: Meets minimum qualification gate with 14+ years of professional experience and BS in Computer Science
Demonstrated ability to optimize performance-critical systems (low-latency infrastructure at Dunnhumby, real-time services)
Shows systems thinking across software-hardware boundaries (C/C++ at National Instruments, embedded work, DevOps infrastructure)
concerns: No evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture experience, or familiarity with SIMD/MIMD systems—core requirements for hardware design feedback
Background is web/mobile/application development, not hardware acceleration or chip-level optimization for AI workloads
Current AI-agent infrastructure work appears to involve LLM workflows, not deep-learning training/inference optimization or neural network acceleration
